web.description: This 650 sqm Riyadh courtyard villa specifies 304 stainless steel cabinetry across 3 zones: family kitchen, outdoor kitchen, and bath vanity.
web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.fadiorhome.com/projects/riyadh-garden-courtyard-villa#main-content)

Project case

Design Study

# Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa

![Fadior Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa — island, courtyard window, walnut cabinet wall, terrazzo floor](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/4li81o9s/production/9ddc9ada9eefd5f95b3a82ce8870dc2833a95c24-1665x944.png?rect=204,0,1259,944&w=1600&h=1200&q=80&fit=max&auto=format)

This 650 sqm Riyadh courtyard villa specifies 304 stainless steel cabinetry across 3 zones: family kitchen, outdoor kitchen, and bath vanity. The package uses 1.5 mm panels, 220 kg load planning, 200000-cycle hardware assumptions, and a 20-year finish warranty for hot-desert family hosting.

By [![Editorial portrait of Marco Rinaldi, Architectural Systems Lead at Fadior Home](https://www.fadiorhome.com/_next/image?url=%2Fauthors%2Fmarco-rinaldi.png&w=3840&q=75&dpl=dpl_CwiDpD6F1B9TBYD4fgcUBsbrZYQS)Marco RinaldiArchitectural Systems Lead](https://www.fadiorhome.com/journal/authors/marco-rinaldi)

Reviewed by [![Editorial portrait of Adriana Hale, Senior Materials Editor at Fadior Home](https://www.fadiorhome.com/_next/image?url=%2Fauthors%2Fadriana-hale.png&w=3840&q=75&dpl=dpl_CwiDpD6F1B9TBYD4fgcUBsbrZYQS)Adriana HaleSenior Materials Editor](https://www.fadiorhome.com/journal/authors/adriana-hale)

PublishedJune 23, 2026/ReviewedJune 23, 2026

VillaRiyadh, Saudi Arabia650 sqm

Project conclusion

The result is a Riyadh villa kitchen system that separates hospitality from service without making either side feel secondary. A guest sees a calm island, courtyard light, and warm storage walls; the household staff.

[View related collection](https://www.fadiorhome.com/collections) [Discuss a similar project](https://www.fadiorhome.com/consultation)

![Fadior Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa — island, courtyard window, walnut cabinet wall, terrazzo floor](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/4li81o9s/production/9ddc9ada9eefd5f95b3a82ce8870dc2833a95c24-1665x944.png?rect=204,0,1259,944&w=1600&h=1200&q=80&fit=max&auto=format)

## What does Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa prove as a Fadior project case?

Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa proves how Fadior can turn a Villa in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia across 650 sqm into a complete, documented stainless steel cabinetry project rather than a loose collection of decorative furniture. The original challenge was specific: Riyadh villas often separate guest reception, family dining, and service work into distinct routes, so the kitchen cannot behave like a single open European room. In. Fadior's response was equally specific: Fadior's response divides the villa into a show kitchen, an outdoor kitchen, and a bath vanity line, all tied by the same 304 stainless steel core. The finished result shows the practical outcome: The result is a Riyadh villa kitchen system that separates hospitality from service without making either side feel secondary. A guest sees a calm island, courtyard. The case gives homeowners, designers, and developers a concrete reference for judging how Fadior moves from brief to material choice, production logic, installation thinking, and lived outcome.

## Why does 304 stainless steel matter in Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa?

304 stainless steel matters in Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa because the cabinetry has to survive real residential conditions in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia: water, humidity, cleaning, storage weight, repeated opening, and long service life. Fadior's material direction for this case centers on 304, brushed, PVD champagne, warm satin, with Fadior whole-home cabinetry systems carrying the visible room function. The important point is that the cabinet body is not MDF, plywood, particle board, or a wood-composite core hidden under a premium surface. Fadior uses 304 stainless steel as the structural base, then applies residential finish systems so the project can look refined without giving up waterproof, zero-formaldehyde, and corrosion-resistant performance. That distinction is especially relevant when kitchens, bathrooms, wardrobes, entryways, or living storage need the same specification logic across the home. In this case, 304 steel turns the design claim into a buildable durability claim.

Project requirements

## The brief behind this reference project, the design response, and the documented outcome.

This 650 sqm Riyadh courtyard villa specifies 304 stainless steel cabinetry across 3 zones: family kitchen, outdoor kitchen, and bath vanity. The package uses 1.5 mm panels, 220 kg load planning, 200000-cycle hardware assumptions, and a 20-year finish warranty for hot-desert family hosting.

Who it's for

## Who this specification is for.

This reference design is for a Riyadh luxury-home owner, villa developer, or family-office procurement lead planning a premium compound residence above the AED or SAR high-net-worth tier. It suits households that host 20-40 guests, keep a separate service kitchen, and need the show kitchen, outdoor cooking counter, and bath vanity to clean quickly after daily staff use.

Material spec

## Material specification and standards.

Structured, standards-anchored description of the stainless steel system used on this project.

Steel grade304

Sheet thickness1.5 mm

Standards referencedASTM A240NSF/ANSI 51HACCPEN 1672-2ISO 14001CARB Phase 2

FinishesbrushedPVD champagnewarm satinmatte cabinet front

Key dimensions

## The numbers behind this specification.

Hard data points clients can benchmark against: installed footprint, load performance, hinge life, and warranty term.

650m²

Installed area

220kg

Load rating

200,000open/close

Hinge cycles

20years

Warranty

Challenge

## Riyadh Villa Heat, Hosting, and 304 Stainless Steel

Riyadh villas often separate guest reception, family dining, and service work into distinct routes, so the kitchen cannot behave like a single open European room. In this 650 sqm villa, the family kitchen, outdoor kitchen, and vanity suite must support 3 daily use patterns without exposing prep traffic to the majlis.

The desert climate changes the specification. Summer temperatures can exceed 45 C, dust builds up quickly, and outdoor counters face thermal movement across a 12-hour day. A Riyadh villa kitchen needs 1.5 mm 304 stainless steel panels, sealed plinths, and ASTM A240 material traceability before decorative finishes are discussed.

Cleaning is also a planning issue, not only a maintenance issue. A 650 sqm family residence may run 2 staff shifts, 7-day hosting, and weekend courtyard meals for 30 guests. Porous surfaces near the outdoor kitchen risk staining, while 304 stainless steel allows food-contact cleaning under NSF/ANSI 51 assumptions.

The bath vanity zone adds another load case because cosmetics, towels, water, and stone counters share one long elevation. The design sets a 220 kg planning load for reinforced base cabinetry and keeps wet items away from the family kitchen storage spine, reducing cross-use clutter across 3 rooms.

The guest path also needs privacy control. A high-walled Riyadh courtyard can screen 2 family entrances, 1 staff route, and 1 outdoor cooking edge, but the cabinetry must keep food, glassware, and vanity storage legible. The plan assigns each zone a clear cleaning sequence before finishes are selected.

Solution

## Riyadh Villa Zones Planned Around PVD 304 Panels

Fadior's response divides the villa into a show kitchen, an outdoor kitchen, and a bath vanity line, all tied by the same 304 stainless steel core. The 1.5 mm panel specification keeps the kitchen readable as one system while allowing different fronts, counter depths, and cleaning expectations in each zone.

The family kitchen uses a large island for plating and coffee service, with tall storage set back from the courtyard doors. A 200000-cycle hardware assumption supports repeated staff access, while HACCP and EN 1672-2 references keep the food-prep logic clear for a home that behaves like a small hospitality operation.

The outdoor kitchen uses the same 304 stainless steel substrate behind warmer visible finishes, so the counter line can handle dust, heat, and frequent wipe-downs. The design reserves 120 cm circulation beside the grill zone and gives the service team a direct path that avoids the dining table.

For the vanity suite, the same material discipline becomes quiet rather than industrial. Reinforced base units carry the 220 kg planning load, while sealed drawer interiors and ventilated plinths help towels and cosmetics stay separated from wet cleaning routines. ISO 14001 and CARB Phase 2 references support the material file.

The three linked spaces share a restrained palette, but the operating logic is measurable. Across 650 sqm, the system defines 3 wet-adjacent zones, 1.5 mm 304 sheet thickness, a 20-year finish warranty, and a food-contact cleaning pathway suitable for private staff and family use.

Finish planning stays subordinate to operation. The island can read warm and residential, the terrace counter can read hospitable, and the vanity can read quiet, but all 3 zones keep the same 304 core, 200000-cycle access assumption, and documented cleaning route for the household team.

Gallery

## Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa — project gallery and key details.

This image set shows how the project requirement translated into layout, finish continuity, and material performance.

![Fadior Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa — counter, cabinetry, tile backsplash, courtyard window](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/4li81o9s/production/9f62925bd16243641ada7fabef812e01042fef54-1536x1024.png?rect=10,0,1517,1024&w=1600&h=1080&q=80&fit=max&auto=format)

![Fadior Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa — dining table, breakfast bar, cabinet wall, terrace glazing](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/4li81o9s/production/bae15c9180ac5d1bc4f2a02a1e505e649f0e561e-1536x1024.png?rect=10,0,1517,1024&w=1600&h=1080&q=80&fit=max&auto=format)

![Fadior Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa — vanity counter, mirror wall, cabinet base, stone floor](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/4li81o9s/production/09055c67b046d03f35e94059062c0d41ddc89fd3-1536x1024.png?rect=10,0,1517,1024&w=1600&h=1080&q=80&fit=max&auto=format)

![Fadior Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa — terrace seating, outdoor counter, courtyard wall, garden planting](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/4li81o9s/production/9b93798e295a90fd1a29c841dea2a6e2285c12f8-1536x1024.png?rect=10,0,1517,1024&w=1600&h=1080&q=80&fit=max&auto=format)

![Fadior Riyadh Garden Courtyard Villa — cabinet face, counter edge, checkerboard tile, terrazzo floor](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/4li81o9s/production/5e11b372a56062dfed0557b1a5220b3e2793cc46-1536x1024.png?rect=10,0,1517,1024&w=1600&h=1080&q=80&fit=max&auto=format)

Result

## Riyadh Villa Durability From 304 Stainless Steel

The result is a Riyadh villa kitchen system that separates hospitality from service without making either side feel secondary. A guest sees a calm island, courtyard light, and warm storage walls; the household staff still gets 3 direct work zones and clear cleaning access after each hosted meal.

The specification also reduces replacement risk in a climate where delicate surfaces age quickly. 304 stainless steel, ASTM A240 material records, and NSF/ANSI 51 food-contact assumptions give the owner a practical basis for cleaning, while the visible room can still carry timber, stone, and warm evening light.

Procurement becomes easier because the kitchen, outdoor counter, and vanity do not require 3 unrelated suppliers. One 20-year warranty file, one finish schedule, and one hardware-cycle standard can cover the villa's wet-adjacent cabinetry, lowering approval friction for the owner, designer, and site contractor.

For a 650 sqm courtyard residence, the value is not a louder kitchen; it is a more controlled house. The family wing gains a show kitchen, the terrace gains a durable outdoor work surface, and the bath suite gains storage that tolerates daily water exposure without losing its refined character.

The owner receives a design file that can move into pricing without re-opening the basic material question. The measurable core is simple: 304 grade, 1.5 mm sheet, 220 kg planning load, 200000-cycle access assumption, and 20 years of finish warranty coverage across the villa's wet-adjacent cabinetry.

For the design team, the case also creates a repeatable Riyadh template. A 3-zone residence can be costed room by room, yet the material file remains unified under ASTM A240, HACCP cleaning logic, and one documented warranty path for approval.

Why stainless steel

## Why 304 stainless steel is the right fit for this project type.

304 stainless steel gives the Riyadh family kitchen a food-contact substrate that can be cleaned after 2 daily staff shifts without swelling or edge failure. ASTM A240 and NSF/ANSI 51 references make the 1.5 mm panel choice auditable for private-villa procurement.

The outdoor kitchen faces dust, heat, and courtyard meals for 20-40 guests, so the material must tolerate repeated wipe-downs. A 304 stainless steel carcass behind warm visible finishes keeps the counter line stable through 45 C summer conditions and evening hosting cycles.

The bath vanity uses the same material for a different reason: water control. Reinforced 304 base units, a 220 kg planning load, and ventilated plinths help the vanity manage towels, cosmetics, and daily cleaning without borrowing kitchen-grade maintenance routines.

Using one grade across 3 zones simplifies warranty and replacement logic. A 20-year finish warranty, 200000-cycle hardware assumption, and ISO 14001 documentation let the owner approve one coherent system instead of three unrelated wet-area packages.

FAQ

## Frequently asked questions about this project.

Is 304 stainless steel suitable for a Riyadh villa kitchen?+

Yes. A Riyadh villa kitchen can use 304 stainless steel when the specification controls sheet thickness, ventilation, and cleaning access. This 650 sqm design uses 1.5 mm panels, ASTM A240 material reference, and NSF/ANSI 51 food-contact assumptions across the family kitchen and outdoor kitchen.

How does the design handle an outdoor kitchen in desert heat?+

The outdoor kitchen uses 304 stainless steel behind warmer visible finishes so dust, heat, and frequent cleaning do not damage the working structure. The design keeps 120 cm circulation near the grill counter and separates service movement from guest dining during 20-40 person courtyard meals.

Why include the bath vanity in the same cabinetry specification?+

The bath vanity shares wet-area risks with the kitchen: water, cosmetics, towels, and daily cleaning. Using the same 304 stainless steel discipline lets the vanity carry a 220 kg planning load, sealed storage, and a 20-year finish warranty without creating a separate procurement file.

Does the villa need a separate service kitchen?+

Many Riyadh compounds separate guest reception from staff preparation, so this specification supports both. The family kitchen remains presentable for hosting, while the service path and outdoor counter keep prep, cleanup, and staff circulation away from the majlis and dining table.

What standards matter for a private villa kitchen package?+

For this private villa, useful references include ASTM A240 for 304 stainless steel sheet, NSF/ANSI 51 for food-contact assumptions, HACCP for cleaning logic, EN 1672-2 for hygienic equipment principles, and ISO 14001 for environmental management documentation during procurement review.

web.title: US Tariff Update 2026 - Dimerco
web.description: Scheduled increases to Section 232 tariffs have been delayed until at least 2027 by Executive Order, maintaining the current 25% tariff on upholstered furniture ...
web.markdown:
###### [Home](https://dimerco.com/) » US Tariff Update 2026 # US Tariff Update 2026 ## Happening Now The White House continues to roll out new global trade policies across key trade partners. This page provides an up-to-date recap of the latest announcements and their potential impact on global trade. ##### June 5, 2026 - 232 Tariff Changes & New Section 301 Tariffs Proposed As you may know, the Administration has made changes to the Section 232 tariffs on copper, aluminum, and steel that are effective Monday, June 8th. The changes mainly impact agricultural equipment, heavy industrial equipment, and HVAC systems and their components, but importers should check the lists to confirm whether their products are affected. One key shift in this Presidential Action is that products may now qualify as US originating if 85% of their components are of US origin vs. the 95% threshold in the previous order. Please see the outline below of the changes and reach out if you have questions or want to confirm the impact of the changes on your products. Keep in mind that CBP has not yet issued guidance on these changes, so we’ll provide updates when the implementing language is released. In addition to the Section 232 changes, the USTR issued a Federal Register Notice that makes clear the Administration’s intentions after the Section 122 10% tariff expires on July 24. The proposal is to implement a 10% or 12.5% Section 301 tariff on all products from the 60 countries subject to the forced labor investigation, with an exemption for products listed in Annex A (mostly the same list of exemptions that exist for Section 122 tariffs today). The new Section 301 tariff would not stack on top of the Section 232 tariffs (unlike the existing China Section 301 tariff). The Administration separately announced the results of the Section 301 case on Brazil, which proposes a 25% tariff on most products from this country (with the same list of exemptions). It is unclear if this Section 301 tariff would stack on top of the 12.5% also proposed for Brazil in the Forced Labor 301 case. ##### Section 232 Steel, Aluminum, and Copper Changes 1. The changes are effective with June 8, 2026 (Monday) arrivals (12:01 EST). 2. Through this order, the Administration is lowering duty rates on agricultural equipment, industrial equipment, and machinery. 3. The changes also expand the list of products that will benefit from a temporary reduction in duty from 25% to a 15% compound rate (base duty rate + 232 tariff at a 15% total maximum) to include ag equipment, HVAC systems, and components. 4. New tariffs are placed on aluminum lithograph plates and steel racks – these weren’t previously subject to 232’s. 5. **The threshold to qualify as US originating is now 85% vs. the former 95% US content.** 6. From June 8, 2026, to December 31, 2027, 232 tariff on goods classified under HTS codes in Annex C (mainly ag and industrial equipment and machinery) is set at 25% except: a. Goods made of metals from Argentina, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Japan, South Korea, Liechtenstein, Switzerland, Taiwan, the UK, and the EU – the 232 tariff is set at a maximum of 15%, including base rate duty. b. Goods made of US metal – the 232 tariff is still set at 10%. c. USMCA-qualifying goods of Canada or Mexico – the 232 tariff applies only to the total value of non-US content, EXCEPT that the total effective tariff rate cannot drop below 15%. 7. The annexes are amended to lower the tariff rate from 25% to a compound total 15% (including base tariff and 232 tariff) for some goods, and add the 232 tariff to the goods listed in #4 above. ##### Section 301 Tariffs Proposed on 60 Countries – Potentially Replacing Section 122 Tariffs - The USTR announced its findings yesterday in its Forced Labor Section 301 cases on 60 countries around the globe. - As a result of the findings, the USTR is proposing **additional duties on all products of these countries except for products listed in its Annex A.** - The USTR determined that 54 of the investigated countries failed to impose and effectively enforce a forced labor import prohibition. **It proposes a 12.5% tariff on these countries**, except for some countries that have signed reciprocal framework agreements with the US (see list below). - It found that 6 of the countries failed to effectively enforce a forced labor import prohibition. **The USTR proposes a 10% Section 301 tariff on these countries (see list below).** - The USTR is also proposing a textile mechanism that would allow for a certain volume of apparel and textile imports from certain countries to enter the US at a reduced Section 301 tariff rate. - The USTR is seeking public comments on the proposed actions and will hold open hearings in early July ( **presumably to have the new tariffs in place when the Section 122 10% tariff expires on July 24th**). - The new Section 301 tariffs would apply to all products from these countries but **would not stack on top of Section 232 tariffs**. Additionally, products in Annex A, as noted above, would be exempt. After a quick review, **Annex A appears to mostly mirror the existing Section 122 exclusion list,** but importers should check their products carefully to confirm status. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Policy Category** | **Applicable Countries / Regions** | **Tariff Rates & Specific Details** | | **Section** **232**
